description: A clinical neuropsychologist has collected Finger Tapping Task (FTT) data to evaluate motor slowing and fatigue. Using the dataset at ~/pebl/data/ftt_tapping_data.csv (containing columns: participant_id, diagnosis, hand, trial, tap_number, tap_time_ms, and iti_ms), calculate the following metrics for each participant: average tapping frequency (Hz) for Dominant and Non-Dominant hands (total taps divided by 10.0 per trial), the Dominant Fatigue Index (the ratio of taps occurring after 5000ms to those occurring at or before 5000ms, averaged across trials), and the Dominant Coefficient of Variation (average of standard deviation divided by mean of iti_ms per trial, excluding the first tap). Exclude participant P099 from the group statistics as a corrupted mechanical profile. Save a JSON report to ~/pebl/analysis/ftt_fatigue_report.json containing a 'participants' list (each with 'id', 'dominant_hz', 'nondominant_hz', 'dominant_fatigue_index', 'dominant_cv', OR 'excluded': true with a 'reason') and a 'group_statistics' dictionary with 'mean_dominant_hz', 'mean_nondominant_hz', 'mean_fatigue_index', and 'mean_cv' for valid participants.
